arts.ttl: Arsenal, Barcelona or Man United? Which club leads the race for Espanyol goalkeeper Joan Garcia?
arts.sum: Arsenal are allegedly at the head of the queue for Espanyol goalkeeper Joan Garcia, but both Manchester United and Barcelona are also said to be interested.

arts.ttl: Arsenal vs PSV Eindhoven – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Arsenal face PSV Eindhoven in the second leg of their Champions League last-16 tonight with a huge advantage. The North Londoners are 7-1 ahead on aggregate after a record-breaking night in Eindhoven last week. Barring the unlikeliest comeback in Champions League history, Arsenal will take their place in the quarter-finals for a second successive season. arts.ttl: Arsenal dream Champions League scenario emerges after Liverpool crash out to PSG
arts.sum: Arsenal face PSV Eindhoven in the- second leg of their Champions League round of 16 tie on March 12. Arsenal look to have been handed a favourable pathway to the Champions League final after Liverpool's shock last-16 defeat to Paris Saint-Germain.

arts.ttl: Arsenal want to resume negotiations in ‘coming weeks’ – Player open to cashing in
arts.sum: Arsenal’s Leandro Trossard has just over 15 months left on his contract and the north London club are looking to offer him an extension. In September, the Belgium international reportedly received an offer from Al-Ittihad. arts.ttl: Report: Arsenal in pole position to beat Barcelona to €30m transfer
arts.sum: Arsenal are reportedly in pole position to beat Barcelona to the transfer of Espanyol goalkeeper Joan Garcia, who has a €30m release clause. The talented Spanish shot-stopper has impressed in La Liga and it seems he’s set to attract plenty of interest this summer. arts.ttl: Ben Foster claims Arsenal's 'under the radar' player is becoming the best in his position in the league
arts.sum: Ben Foster has lauded the performance of one of Mikel Arteta’s best players following his display in Arsenal’s 1-1 draw with Manchester United. However, the...

We have a 100% record in the Champions League this term on our own patch without conceding, putting us on the brink of hitting six-straight wins row in Europe's premier club competition for the first time since 2005, setting us up perfectly for a visit from either of Spain's capital clubs. We head into the second leg of our Champions League last-16 tie with PSV Eindhoven with a commanding 7-1 lead, knowing our spot in the quarter-finals is all-but secure.
